rulings.Case Title: MARY ANN MILLER VS. DARCY L WALLACE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 23, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT DARCY WALLACE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion granted. Service on defendant was not made within two years of the complaint being filed - CCP section 583.410 and 583.420(a)(1). Plaintiff fails to show good cause why the case should not be dismissed. Plaintiff has not been diligent in prosecuting this complaint. The facts underlying this action are more than seven years old, and the probate estate was closed in Marin almost six years ago. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH LUI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 23, 2010, line 15, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication granted as to retailiation claim, no triable issue of material fact. Gonzales declaration states other candidate received language liaison position based on merit and plaintiff presents no substantial responsive evidece of pretext. Summary adjudication denied as to remaining causes of action. Triable issues of fact raised as to disability discrimination: whether plaintiff suffered an adverse employment action in that he was forced out when Sullivan told him his only option was to seek disability retirement. (Plft's declaration pargraphs 49-52.) Triable issue of fact whether plaintiff can perform the essential functions of a sworn police officer. (Cripe vs. City of San Jose (2001-9th Cir.) 261 F. 3d 877.) Plaintiff's evidence in the form of officer declarations create triable issue of fact whether making a forcible arrest is an essential function of the police position. Summary adjudication denied as to reasonable accommodation: factual issue raised as to whether defendant made sufficient efforts to reasonably accommodate plaintiff. Plaintiff's declaration at paragraph 43 states his captain said he could have his station keeper job back, and the record is not clear that "bumping" would have occurred. Summary adjudication denied as to interactive process claim: triable issue of fact raised whether Sullivan was the problem with the process. Summary adjudication denied as to failure to prevent discrimination, triable issues of fact raised regarding disability discrimination. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS A. VIERA VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 23, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, OFFICER GOLDSOBOROUGH, OFFICER SANDERS, OFFICER HASTINGS, LIEUTENANT SILVERMAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the 8th, 9th, 10th and 11th causes of action. Public employment is not held by contract but by statute. (Miller vs. State of California (1977) 18 Cal.3d 808). The doctrine of estoppel by agency may not be applied to hold the department liable. (Boren vs. State Personnel Board (1951) 37 Cal.2nd 634.) The San Francisco Police Department is dismissed as it is not a proper party in this action. (Darby vs. Pasadena Police (5th Cir. 1991) 939 F.2d 311.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT BARTAL VS. MIKE PRYZVEIZSKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Motion For Orders Determining Deft The Prevailing Party And Awarding Attorneys Fees To Deft; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 23, 2010, line 11, DEFENDANT MIKE PRYZVEIZSKI Motion For Orders Determining Defendant the Prevailing Party and Awarding Attorneys Fees To Defendant. Motion granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted to declare defendant the prevailing party. Motion for attorney fees denied. No finding has been made that plaintiff has violated Penal Code section 290.46(1)(4)(A). SFAC 37.10B(c)(6) is unconstitutional and a violation of the Equal Protection Clause, in that it authorizes attorney fees to a single class of litigants or in a single class of cases. (See City of Alturas vs. The Superior Court of Modoc Country (1940) 36 Cal.App.2d 457. =(302/CWW)
